source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - MC 778 A Structural Damage and Leak

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 778

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Hydraulic Fluid and Well Fluid Release
actions_taken: Process Shut Down (PSD) activated by gas detector alarm
SCSSV re-opened and hydraulic control lines re-pressurized from 23-25 October 2020
Main generators put back on-line at 21:30
Pressure bled off in tubing above SCSSV on 31 October 2020
SV1 tree isolation valve closed by ROV
Samples taken from SCSSV control line vent on 5 November 2020
Affected gas detectors removed, replaced, and sent for testing
event.summary: On 22 October 2020 at 13:59, MC 778 A (Thunder Horse) experienced an unplanned power outage when line-of-sight gas detectors near the air compressor header activated, triggering a Process Shut Down and subsequent Emergency Platform Shut Down. Upon restart and troubleshooting, loss of hydraulic fluid was discovered at Subsea Well MC 776 TG002, caused by leaking SCSSV rod piston seals. An estimated 3 to 50 barrels of Transaqua Hydraulic Control fluid and/or hydrocarbon entered the Gulf of Mexico. The SCSSV was re-opened and hydraulic control lines were re-pressurized to prevent further loss of containment. ROV inspection confirmed no fluid flowing from the SCSSV vent. A function test on 21 November 2020 confirmed the SCSSV failed to function open. The well is currently secure with subsea tree valves in the closed position.
recommendations: Raise awareness for SCSSV rod piston seal failures in Gulf
Encourage operators to report known SCSSV rod piston seal failures to BSEE under 30 CFR 250.803

Encourage operators to train Control Room Operators to identify rod piston seal failures by monitoring irregular hydraulic pump cycling, irregular hydraulic fluid consumption, and abnormal hydraulic pressure
Before evacuating for hurricanes, operators should bleed all flowlines sub-ambient to prevent rod piston leaks from causing well fluid loss of containment
Explore opportunities for Best Available and Safest Technology (BAST) around mitigating pollution risk from sub-sea hydraulic venting

threats.name: SCSSV rod piston seal degradation and failure
Loss of hydraulic pressure in control system
Gas detector malfunction or false alarm
Unplanned power outage to air compressor system

consequences.name: Release of hydraulic fluid to marine environment
Release of well hydrocarbon fluids to marine environment
Unplanned platform shutdown and loss of production

controls.name: Line-of-sight gas detectors near air compressor header
controls.barrier_role: Detection of gas release to activate shutdown
controls.lod_basis: Early detection system to prevent escalation

supporting_text: line-of-sight gas detectors near the air compressor header activated the Control Room (CR) alarm and a PSD occurred
The line-of-sight gas detectors were suspected of failing. There were no leaking gases detected at that location.
The affected detectors were removed and replaced and sent it for testing.

controls.name: Process Shut Down (PSD) system triggered by gas detector alarm
controls.barrier_role: Automatic shutdown of air compressor to isolate hazard
controls.lod_basis: Automatic response to detected gas alarm

supporting_text: line-of-sight gas detectors near the air compressor header activated the Control Room (CR) alarm and a PSD occurred

controls.name: Emergency Platform Shut Down (ESD) system triggered by Pressure Safety Low (PSL) on air supply
controls.barrier_role: Automatic emergency shutdown of main generators and platform systems
controls.lod_basis: Secondary automatic shutdown system to prevent escalation

supporting_text: The Pressure Safety Low (PSL) on the air supply resulted in an ESD and personnel muster
At 14:20, CR operators could not find the fault before the automatic activation of the ESD. This signal also shut down the main generators.

controls.name: SCSSV (Surface Control Subsurface Safety Valve) rod piston seals
controls.barrier_role: Seal to prevent well fluid and hydraulic fluid mixing and leakage
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical seal to contain pressurized fluids

supporting_text: indications of loss of hydraulic fluid was discovered at Subsea Well MC 776 TG002
Further troubleshooting indicated a leaking SCSSV (Surface Control Subsurface Safety Valve) rod piston seals
The probable cause of the incident is equipment failure of the SCSSV rod piston
Equipment Failure: Inoperable equipment or safety devices - The SCSSV rod piston seals leaked.

controls.name: Hydraulic fluid reservoir level monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detection of hydraulic fluid loss through visual inspection
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection method to identify seal failures

supporting_text: Upon the restart of the facility and troubleshooting the event, indication of loss of hydraulic fluid was detected at Well MC 776 TG002. This was shown by a lower amount of hydraulic fluid in the reservoir.

controls.name: Hydraulic pump cycling and pressure monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detection of seal failures through abnormal system behavior
controls.lod_basis: Operator monitoring to identify irregular patterns indicating seal failure

supporting_text: Encourage operators to train CRO's to identify rod piston seal failures by monitoring irregular hydraulic pump cycling, irregular hydraulic fluid consumption, and abnormal hydraulic pressure.

controls.name: Subsea tree isolation valves (production master valve, production wing valve, production isolation valve)
controls.barrier_role: Isolation of wellbore to prevent further fluid release
controls.lod_basis: Primary containment barrier to isolate well fluids

supporting_text: The SCSSV is currently in the closed position with the well secure with the subsea tree valves (production master valve, production wing valve, and production isolation valve) in the closed position
The ROV was used to close the SV1 tree isolation valve, which provides isolation of the wellbore to the A side of the SCSSV.

controls.name: Pressure bleed-off procedure before hurricane evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Reduce pressure in flowlines to sub-ambient to prevent leakage during shutdown
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to mitigate risk during extended shutdowns

supporting_text: At approximately 17:55 on 31 October 2020 after communications were established with the well, the pressure was bled off in the tubing above the SCSSV
Before evacuating for hurricanes, operators should bleed all flowlines sub-ambient to prevent rod piston leaks from causing well fluid loss of containment

On 26 October 2020, the well was shut-in in preparation for Hurricane Zeta evacuation. Review of pressure data for the event indicates a potential risk for additional leak through the vent line following the shut-in on 26 October 20.

controls.name: ROV inspection and visual verification of vent line
controls.barrier_role: Verification that no fluid is actively leaking from SCSSV vent
controls.lod_basis: Secondary verification to confirm containment status

supporting_text: In addition, a vessel was deployed to the field on 1 November 2020 for ROV inspection of the well. The ROV video footage showed no fluid was flowing from the SCSSV vent.

controls.name: Pressure integrity testing of isolation valves
controls.barrier_role: Verification of valve sealing integrity to confirm containment
controls.lod_basis: Testing to confirm secondary containment barriers are functional

supporting_text: Pressure integrity testing of the closed SV1 isolation valve resulted in an increase in the return pressure in the vent line when pressure was bled but fluids were not observed exiting the vent.

controls.name: Leak rate testing of SCSSV
controls.barrier_role: Quantification of any residual leakage to confirm containment

supporting_text: A leak rate test was conducted on the SCSSV on 1 December 2020 which resulted in a 0 cc/minute recorded leak rate.

controls.name: Auxiliary generator backup power system
controls.barrier_role: Restoration of power to safety systems following main generator shutdown
controls.lod_basis: Secondary power source to restore critical safety functions

supporting_text: Next, the auxiliary generator was brought on-line and the safety system was restored. The platform safety system was back in-service by 14:25.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
